concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c concept oceConcept-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
concept.title: Limitations / Known problems of software version R4.4.0.0

entry.p: Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Restart of printer needed prior to re-install with keep settings to keep accounting data of the day.
Restart the printer before the re-install.
During an upgrade of the operator panel firmware, the screen remains black for 5 minutes without any user feedback.
No option to switch off during installation wizard.
During the first steps of the installation wizard, the operator panel does not provide an option to cancel the wizard (e.g. to shutdown the system).
Proceed to the step "configure date and time". In that step the cancel button is available.
Press on cancel and then the user interface is available.
Impossible to enter a NSO SOID with dash.
When entering the SOID, only enter the numbers
Upgrade via ORS may be blocked if window is closed/re-opened.
The upgrade from WUI or from BackOffice is made in 3 steps: first download the packages, then install, and at the end upgrade after restart of the system.

If during the installation (progress bar on WUI from 0 to100%), the WUI session is closed (browser is closed): the installation is still running, so wait for 1hour and after this delay, restart the printer.
At start-up, the upgrade will be made.
Not clear for a user when the base license has been received.
License availability can take up till 15 minutes.
This process can be speed up by opening the "Remote connection" menu on LUI, and pressing "Test remote connection"; this enforces an ORS connection.
Base license via ORS; Restart message is shown at the wrong moment.
Select "Install license from remote server" to install the license, and then press "Install" in the popup.
A popup is shown with the message "Restart the printer to implement the changes". This is not correct; at this stage the printer does not have to be restarted.
First the Base license must be installed. This is performed after pressing OK.
When this has been done, the printer should indeed be restarted, but at this stage no restart message is shown.
If you now start printing, a license error is reported when the print is finished.
Installation wizard does not appear anymore after restart due to MRE.
Restart again the complete system.
11520 after installation executed with KeepSettings=No
or
engine switched off after installation executed with KeepSettings=No.
When installing a system via USB with the KeepSettings=No, the system will end the installation in a situation where the system looks off (screen of the operator panel is black).
Recover the system:

li.p: Switch off the engine with the main switch (22S1) on the power box.

entry.p: After software upgrade, the ORS connection is lost.
When customer has DHCP server present and DHCP is disabled on machine.
Restart the system.
White bottle not shown on User Interface after installation
After running the SDS test to indicate the installation if the white ink components has been completed, it takes a controller reboot to show the 5th white bottle in the UI.
The white print modes will already be supported if also the associated white ink license in installed.
Additonal reboot required to show white bottle in User Interface
"During installation of an R4.3 engine, the controller software might notice that there is an update of the firmware for the UI.
An update of this firmware will be started without further notice.
UI will be black for about 5 minutes.
After completion of the update the screen will show up again.
This happens with user panels with old firmware and is not likely to occur with the R4 Machines in the Field."
In an exceptional situation the orientation of the UI could be wrong or the touch screen could be deactivated.
In that case the engine has to be restarted.
After this restart the touch screen will be working correctly.
Orientation will be correct after about 2 minutes.
Restart
Installation wizard: Test failed (because still in Service mode ?)
Installation wizard: after resuming the wizard (either with Resume in Tools or after a restart) the wizard can start with ("Test failed").
This happens because engine is not yet in warm mode.
Try again.
Installation wizard: during first installation tissue cassette tile can be red but replace button is grayed out
Installation wizard: During first installation, the tissue cassette tile can be red, but the replace button is grayed out.
It can be replaced anyway:
Open the cover, place the cassette and then the engine will update its status after closing the cover.
Iinstallation Wizard does not pop up when only embedded is restarted (after an MRE)
If an engine MRE occurs, then embedded software is restarted after pressing "Restart".
The installation wizard will only pop up after selecting "Resume" via the red tile.
Installation Wizard: some strings are in English
Some strings in the wizard are not translated from English:

li.p: 'Please wait' (in the 'Checking calibration status' screen only)

entry.p: During first installation tissue cassette tile can be red but replace button is grayed out
Installation wizard: During first installation, the tissue cassette tile can be red, but the replace button is grayed out.
It can be replaced anyway: Open the cover, place the cassette and then the engine will update its status after closing the cover.
Printhead calibration not retriggered after printhead calibration
Installation wizard: When Printhead calibrations are successful, and a Printhead is replaced afterwards, then then it is possible to finish the installation wizard after executing the engine
Printhead calibrations need to be performed afterwards, via Tools instead of the installation wizard.
Missing Reboot option in installation wizard after ignoring it first
After selecting Back and Cancel in the installation wizard, once a reboot is offered.
Perform a manual reboot needs to be executed before continuing it. This prevents bad configuration of network and/or white-ink.
Error "it is not possible to establish a test session"
In rare cases an error "It is not possible to establish a test session" is displayed after resuming the wizard.
A hard power off/on cycle is sometimes required to resolve the situation.
Installation Wizard status bar placed too low (you can see the M-series label)
In rare cases it will occur that the status bar will cover input fields of the installation wizard.
Cancel and resume wizard.
MRE during installation wizard is followed by Error 11504
After entering zero SDS from splash screen, don't use the Resume button from Tools to resume the wizard.
Use Restart instead.
Installation Wizard does not pop up when only embedded is restarted (after an MRE).
If an engine MRE occurs, then embedded software is restarted after pressing "Restart".
The installation wizard will only pop up after selecting "Resume" via the red tile.
Instruction for when user do not have license number.
Installation wizard: If license number is not available at that moment, it is possible to skip this step by just press "Next".
License number can later be filled in manually.
Some strings are only in English.
Installation wizard: Some strings in the wizard are not translated from English.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
The machine will not go to Standby when there is media on the print surface and the green button is pressed.
Also applies when the Automatic shutdown timer is used.
By design.
No status feedback after restart during 50 seconds.
Wake-up timers behavior can be confusing.
Going to scheduled keep healthy is only done after engine is in low power mode. As low power timer is 90 minutes, going to keep healthy can be done till 1h30 after the expected time.
On engine disconnection during printing system cannot be restarted (from UI MRE)
Use main switch.
Engine indicates ready for at least 1 min after wake up from Keep Healthy until actually it starts waking up & shows initializing... etc.

When waking up the engine while it is still is going to lower power, the user interface might show the engine status "Ready" while it is actually still going to lower power. After the engine reaches lower power, it will start initializing and the status at the user interface will be OK again.
No progress bar on new type UI3 panel.

The new UI3 does not show a progress bar during startup; this is due to limitations of the newly used display controller. After startup, the Windows progress icon is now shown (rolling circle with dots).
Colorado M-series wakes up from keep-healthy takes longer than in the C1650
Maintenance done by tissue wiper unit takes more time compared to the rubber wiper of Colorado 1630/1650.
Hereby reaching standby when coming from Keep Healthy/Low Power consumes more time (+- 1:30 minute)
Machine does sometimes not power off
When a Power off is requested and an MRE is reported during the shutdown phase, the Power off will be blocked.
Only after a timeout period of 20 minutes, the system will shut down.
Power off the machine via the power switch.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Complete qwerty keyboard is shown for entering numeric value on the user interface.
Select the numeric keyboard before entering values.
Assigning media not possible during printing or print is started.
Pause the queue before loading the media or wait for the end of the first job.
After first job is printed, media request will popup.
Cut after each copy doesn't work after loading roll.
Output mode set at the time jobs are received by the controller cannot be changed anymore to "AutoCut".
First set the Output mode to "AutoCut" and then submit the jobs.
Unreliable prediction time on 2 productive jobs moved to top.

When 2 jobs are printed productively with trailing edge in RIP, predictions for the second job is longer than the first one because second job prediction takes trailing edge time of the first one into account.
Print queue on the operator panel does not reflect that media targeted by submitted jobs has been deleted.
When a media is deleted on the system, and there are still jobs using that media on the system, this is not visualized in the queue on LUI.
Active jobs with not-calibrated media do not show the warning icon.
"Wind the output" jumps to default value "Yes" after error recovery (MRE)
Active jobs with not-calibrated media do not show the warning icon.
"Wind the output" jumps to default value "Yes" after error recovery (MRE)
Colorado M-series printer warming up takes longer than in the C1650

Printhead maintenance for Colorado M-series is done with 2 heads at the same time. For the Colorado 1630 and 1650 all heads are maintained at the same time. Hereby the initialization time of the Colorado takes slightly more time.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
"Cut after each copy" does not work for media that requires tight winding or locked tension bar or media not suitable to be cut.
"Cut after each copy" does not work for prints send from the operating panel or SDS.
The warning icon is for un-calibrated media is only shown for jobs with media that is actually loaded in the machine.
The "End of roll" icon does not appear after loading a roll, if the new roll is too short for the job.
When "save last received job" is enabled, you can get 21703 when submitting several jobs rapidly (Example from Caldera).
For normal use, this setting has to be disabled.
Engine starts warming up while print surface temperature is set to 28°C.

When printing a print quality chart from the local UI, first a print is made in specialty then a print is made in high quality. The print platen temperature is according to the temperature in de media catalog. When the print platen temperature has been changed, it is kept on the same value until a new temperature is required by a next job.
Paper step change not taken into account as expected.
Some media settings in the RIP are applied at print time; this means that the new setting (e.g. paper step correction) is used when a new print is started.

This does however not work when a print is re-sent while the previous print is still being printed. In this case the two prints will be handled as one single sequence, so the modified media setting is then not used.
Wait until all prints are finished, and then re-send the print job.
When the "Output configuration" is set to AutoCut, the trailing strip, which can be configured on the operator panel, is not taken into account.
Cut after sheet does not cut with pdf files with 2 pages.
Send separate files via RIP when printing multi page pdf that you want to have cut after each copy automatically.
Productive printing: deletion of second job results into abort printing and reprint of first job.

In productive mode, when you cancel a job while the print of the previous one is in its last 50 cm, the print of this previous one is aborted. The print (of the previous one) is then automatically resumed by the printer.
Job time prediction is not shown for "cut after each copy" jobs.
For "Cut after each copy" jobs, the job time prediction cannot be calculated reliably.
Print strategy cannot be changed per job.
The Print Strategy is taken into account at print time, at the moment a print sequence is started.
As soon as the print sequence is finished, the new value for the setting is taken into account by the system.
Wait until all jobs in the queue are ready or started.
Double-sided printing: distance between jobs can vary.
If during printing, the media has a standstill of more than 10 seconds, it will be transported an additional 2 cm.
It is recommended to create one job in the RIP.
Job with media that "Requires manual loading" is not printed and stays on top of the queue, without any indication why it is not printed.
This RIP setting is especially used for soft signage media.
As long as the feed is not performed, the job stays in the queue.
The user should perform this feed.
Media must be cut and is completely retracted when wrong media width is entered.
In tight winding, media must be cut and completely retracted if entered width is wrong.
Media request when making second job with media that requires manual loading after a cut.
Manual loading requires a leading edge of rigid material on the weak medium.
In order to use this media again, the operator has to reload the roll again, with the rigid material header.
Print on media that requires manual loading results in error 5706.
Printing on a loose-winding media that is configured as "Requires manual loading", results in error 5706.
Use this setting only in combination with tight winding or locked tension bar.
Request to tape media after stopping job with AutoCut.
When cancelling a free-fall job a taping request will occur when the leading edge of the media reaches the taping position.
The taping request can be ignored: the job is cancelled and the taping request will be removed automatically.
The operator always has to enable the checkbox “Specify Physical Width” in the Onyx Layout Preview. This setting is now by default enabled in the new driver.
No job time indicated when AutoCut is overruled
When AutoCut is selected, no print time estimations are shown. This is also the case when AutoCut is selected, but overruled in case of tight winding jobs.
Indicated print time is too low
The indicated print time is too low; the maintenance actions are not yet taken into account.
Changing markers is not taken into account for jobs in the queue
The operator sends a number of jobs to the machine, but gets the error that the markers cannot be read. Printing will be stopped due to this error.
The operator then decides to change the markers from Normal to Enhanced and then resumes printing.
Then the image will be clipped with 8.6 mm on the left side when left alignment was used, or on the right side when right alignment was used.
Reason for this is that the margins are not re-calculated for the jobs in the queue.
In order to take the new margins for the changed markers into account, the jobs have to be deleted from the queue and re-sent.
Jobs are not printed anymore (move to top trouble and media request not shown)
If after a MoveToTop directly another MoveToTop is requested, although the queue shown on the UI does reflect the requested order, this might not reflect the actual printing order.
The longer the queue, the more time is needed to process the first MoveToTop , and the higher the chance this situation to occur.
When this happens, the result can be either:

li.p: Printing progress will be shown to a job further down the UI queue, or
If the first job of the actual printing order has a media request, this will not be visible in the UI and printing will stop (scenario in this bug)
A 11504 crash when performing really quick multiple move-2-tops.

entry.p: A work-around to prevent this situation is to wait a few seconds after the first MoveToTop , before initiating a next MoveToTop. NOTE: 'few seconds' depends on the total length of the queue.
Measurements shows that this time-window of getting into this problem in normal cases is <0.3 secs even for queues of 15 jobs.
Waiting a few seconds should be enough
A work-around to solve:

li.p: Do a new MoveToTop on the printing job in the queue
Do a new MoveToTop on any job except the first / top one

entry.p: Fotoba marks misalignment due to spit bars
When printing right aligned the position of the Fotoba barcode & of the vertical mark is influenced by the presence or absence of the spitbar.
If a printmode requires the spitbar, the Fotoba barcode & the vertical mark are positioned 1.5cm to the left vs when a printmode doesn't require a spitbar.

In order to prevent too many manual adjustments of the Fotoba sensor/knives, recommendation is either to group jobs that require spitbar together (reduce number of changes across a roll) or use a 1.5cm offset when printing the jobs that do not require a spitbar.
All white modes and FLXfinish+ require the spitbar, the others don't.
Slight image loss on right side
When a job is printed right-aligned with Automatic Advance Correction (AAC) set to Normal, an image loss of 1-2mm on the right side occurs.
Workaround: use Enhanced markers or shift the image 2mm to the left in the RIP.
Cancel printing job does not stop printing
Two or more identical jobs are in the queue and the printer is printing productively (without retracting the media in between jobs).
Cancel the active job by pressing the X.
Expected result: printing of the active job stops
Observed result: printing of the active job continues
Press Pause and then press Stop to stop the active job.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
When jobs length is less than 50 cm and they are on top of the queue, it won't be possible to use the 'move to top' functionality on these specific jobs.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Auto-cut mode: every copy results in an entry in the account log file.
SYSTEM jobs, printed_area and active_time, are not completely logged.
Onyx ink estimations in HighSpeed mode not OK.

The ink estimation in the RIP for the gloss max speed and high speed modes can deviate considerably from actual ink use (factor 2). An estimate of the total ink consumption in the glossy high speed mode can be obtained by ripping the same image in the gloss high quality mode, although deviations can still occur (e.g. mono color prints). Alternatively, a print can be made and the actual ink use can be reviewed in the account logging.
Invalid and empty accountlog file is created after installation
After installation, an empty accountlog .csv file is generated that starts with serial number 000000000.
This file is not used and can be ignored.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Dot in gmail address is not shown in cloud service administration portal.
Example: tester.colorado@gmail.com is shown as testercolorado@gmail.com.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
No remote alert when there is a media request or cut request.
Mobile: there is no feedback when first job to be printed is in media or cut request.
Rational is that operator will always go to the engine when starting print so he does not need to be triggered. He will be triggered by the app. As soon as he needs to go after first job is started.
Due to the new setup of the cloud services, the current PRISMAsync Remote Control app (a.k.a Mobile Alerting) does not work anymore in combination with R3.0
A new app is being developed, but this will only be available in Q2-2021.
Lubricators dry is shown in red on Mobile Alert app, but is not blocking. Not aligned with User Panel. (Orange).
PRISMAsync Remote Control does not work anymore after s/w upgrade without Restore.
When a machine is reinstalled from scratch, without performing a Restore, PRISMAsync Remote Control does not work anymore for this machine.
Because the cloud GUID is not restored, the cloud administration considers this as a new machine.
Remove machine from the cloud service administration portal, and then add it again.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Start calibrations for loose winding media always without media on the print surface to make sure that leading edge of media is well calibrated.
The media calibration does not adjust the carriage height. The carriage height is solely determined by the defined media thickness in the RIP.
Make sure to enter the correct media thickness in the RIP, otherwise the print quality will be unsatisfactory.
The AAC signal can be unreliable within the first 0.5 m of the leading edge.
Within the first 0.5 m of the leading edge, the AAC (yellow markers controller) will only apply the step correction, stored from the previous print.
As a consequence, there is no AAC correction in case there is no previous print (Example every time a medium is freshly loaded).
Leading edge media calibration is not performed for non-cuttable media.
When a media calibration is performed for a loose-winding media that is not suitable for Printer/Cutter, the leading edge calibration part is not performed.
This can lead to paper step artefacts in the leading edge of prints.
[procede test] Paperstep visible @ leading edge of the print.
Loose winding media should always be calibrated from the leading edge (coming from the drawer) to make sure the print quality is optimal on the first 50cm.
If a medium has never been calibrated this way (e.g. by using the feed function and then calibrating) it will use a default paper step and print quality might not be optimal.
After machine restart with AutoCut enabled: no AutoCut after media calibration
"Output configuration" is set to "AutoCut".
Restart the machine (normal Restart or Restart after an MRE).
Start a media calibration.
Expected behavior: the media is delivered in free fall and cut after the calibration is finished.
Actual behavior: the media is wound and not automatically cut after the calibration has finished.
After restart, select "Wind the output" : "No" and press "Save".
Then select "AutoCut" again and press "Save".
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
No proper user feedback when no suitable roll is loaded for maintenance/calibration.
The following maintenance/calibration procedures can only be performed on media that is suitable for engine calibration:

li.p: Automatic nozzle optimization in Periodic, Manual or Automatic maintenance

entry.p: In case the loaded roll is not suitable for engine calibration, the roll is not indicated at all when starting one of these procedures.
Load a media that is suitable for engine calibration.
Operator panel screen enlarged after cleaning the screen.
Cleaning the screen can best be performed when UI is off, via soft on/off button. Then clean screen and turn on again.
Be aware that this not always works.
Restart the engine.
Maintenance unit counter error 7703
Waste bin replacements more often required than planned due to:
Reduction max. liquid level waste bin to deal with waste counter uncertainties
Larger purge volume for CYMK than planned, will be optimized in R4.1
More purge wipes needed than planned
Nozzle optimization not possible when winding is enabled
Nozzle optimization contains new testcharts that make print-cure-scan necessary. This makes winding not possible anymore.
Nozzle optimization can only be done when winding is disabled.
Amount of ink waste in tray changed overnight from 78% to 69%

Ink waste percentage displayed on UI is the ratio of software estimated ink waste volume and total allowed ink waste volume (647 ml). The ink waste percentage will increase till 100%, after this the costumer is forced to replace the maintenance tray (red tile). After ink waste percentage is 82% a message "you should replace the maintenance tray" will be displayed (orange tile). The software ink waste volume will be checked and "calibrated" via an ink waste sensor located at the side of the maintenance tray, when the sensor becomes active the software ink waste volume is set to 447ml (69%).
The ink waste percentage on UI can change suddenly from 70-82% to 69% when:

li.p: The ink waste sensor becomes active. When sensor becomes active the ink waste percentage is calibrated to 447 ml = 69% of total capacity of 647 ml. The estimate is in this case higher than calibration volume.

The software estimated ink waste volume becomes higher than 531 ml without waste sensor is active, in this "almost full" level we calibrate the waste percentage again to 447 ml (69%). Tile becomes orange, replacement of maintenance tray is triggered with the message "you should replace the maintenance tray". On UI the ink waste percentage remains 69% till ink waste sensor becomes active. After sensor becomes active the ink waste percentage will increase again to 100%.

The UI displays a "Maintenance sensor is broken" when the software estimated ink waste volume becomes higher than 600 ml without waste sensor is active, error 7923 (maintenanceTrayLevelSensorDefect) will be reported, no access anymore to replace a maintenance tray. From this point 2 cases are possible:

In case the estimated ink waste volume becomes higher than 800 ml and the sensor remains inactive then the tile becomes red and the engine will be blocked and service has to visit to solve the problem (real broken sensor or false positive).

In case the sensor becomes active before the estimated ink waste volume becomes 800 ml then error 7923 will be reset. Costumer will be forced to replace the waste bin (red tile), error 7927 (maintenanceTrayLevelSensorDefectAutoReset) will be reported.

entry.p: False dabbing detection during manual/periodic maintenance with or without soaking single printhead
For R4.2 false dabbing detection(s) during each manual/periodic maintenance occurs and will be logged in datalog counters, only the annoying beeps are gone for the operator.
MaintenanceTrayEstimate restored wrongly
During restoring a backup, the ink level of the maintenance tray will be overwritten with the value of the backup in case this value is higher compared to the current level.
This will then cause a jump in the maintenance tray level, shown on the UI.
To correct this you must replace the maintenance tray by the maintenance procedure triggered from the UI.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Operator panel does not report immediately that non-genuine ink is used.
When non-genuine ink is used, operator panel does not show this as long as the handle is not moved up.
Refilling ink is not possible (LEDs are off) even ink level is 0%.
Refilling a new bottle is only allowed when the drain time (15 min) of the previous bottle has passed.
This is not visible on the operator panel.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
A Colorado print mode has multiple settings. Colorado only supports one definition of a print mode (one set of settings) per media profile.
ONYX supports multiple definitions of a print mode per media profile
If multiple definitions of a print mode in one media profile are synchronized with the Colorado printer, it is unclear which settings will be used by the Colorado printer.
Only use one print mode definition per media profile. If you need multiple definitions then make a separate media profile for each definition.
File conflict error pop-up when editing media.
Restart ONYX when errors occur during editing of media properties after the Onyx Rip Queue and Onyx media manger has been running for longer than one day.
Cannot change print surface temperature in media catalogue

Multiple instances of a printmode, with different printmode settings isn't possible in the current media synchronization architecture between RIP and Printer. Only one instance of a print mode is synchronized and overrides the others.
Create different medias in the RIP to secure the different setting (like print surface temperature)
Wrong preview on user panel for sent job
In some rare cases, Onyx sends the wrong print preview. This means that the thumbnail for the job is not correct.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
After a clean software re-installation (without "Keep settings" or Restore), the Caldera media cannot be used anymore.
They have disappeared from the engine.
Caldera reports an invalid media ID when trying to edit them.
In Easy Media, select "Printer Media", then press the option "Export all Medias from RIP to Printer".
Caldera: RIP stays "Initializing" after editing media in EasyMedia
The following problem can occur now and then:
Scenario:
RIP is started and Colorado M-series window is open.
Open EasyMedia and edit a media.
Quit EasyMedia.
Double-click the "Colorado M-series" driver.
The Colorado M-series window opens, but the status stays "Initializing" forever. The driver does not connect with the printer
Select Settings > ServerAdmin and press "Stop Server".
This will stop the server. After that, the printer driver can be started again.
Advice:
First close the printer driver interface before opening EasyMedia.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Maximum load pin curing: 4 hours.
A single print should not take more than 4 hours.
Engine does not stop when paper touches occur, damaging prints and printheads.

Engine does not stop when paper touches occur. For reasons of productivity it is decided to continue the printing process when media touches occur. Heavy curled media can trigger the side flaps of the curing unit. In these cases the print process will stop with an error.
Tackiness paper applications. Back of paper sticks to print.
For some media we have seen that the printed area sticks to the backside of the next print.
Lower the amounts of ink (IR80% or lower) and/or - lower the print surface temperature to 21°C - use a medium with a different backside.
Gloss difference/banding MATT Quality mode for media with tension bar.
Tension bar gives sometimes print quality artefacts on matt prints when used. Switch to gloss mode, higher print mode or different media.
Color shift within the swath goes from magenta to grey
In general, print made in the print modes 'Max Speed', 'High Speed' or 'Production' can show color banding. Depending on the carriage direction the color changes per print swath.
Gloss banding in Matte High Quality mode prints

If gloss banding occurs in Matte High Quality mode prints, first the media calibration has to be repeated by the operator. If gloss banding still occurs, one of the alternative print strategies can be chosen.
Prints show gloss banding
In the 8-pass print mode, gloss difference per swath can occur. The effect Is highly media dependent (wide films).
Change the print strategy or use slower print mode.
Several cases of Massive DN-classifications

Deviating nozzles (DN's) can occur during mono colour prints, in the colours which are not being used. This should not lead to print quality issues. This should be kept in mind when looking at the nozzle logging.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
The spit bar requires a width of 15mm, so this reduces the maximum printable width in FLXfinish+ and White multilayer prints with 15mm.
FLXfinish+ and multi layer white uses a so-called spit bar to fire the non-used nozzles. This bar is printed at the right side of the image, between the image and the AAC markers.
Caldera automatically takes this into account.
In Onyx the user has to define specific media sizes for FLXfinish+, taking into account the additional required 15mm.
FLXfinish+ and white print color deviations compared to PDF after ripping in Onyx
Combinations of spot color objects and transparent objects can cause color management issues in the RIPs (e.g. color deviations in areas containing such objects).

The color deviations can be caused by complex elements such as transparencies, blends, meshes. They disappear when the files are simplified - e.g. saved as PDF 1.3 which does not support transparencies.
Other potential workarounds include:
- Rasterizing the images upfront, then add/preserve the Spot 2 data to highlight the gloss elements
- Flattening the files with preflight tooling.
FLXfinish+ and white print deviations: gloss area not printed
Sometimes, even if there are some objects/elements in the input file defining the gloss areas, the print out shows one of the following issues:
- print is entirely matte, no gloss areas; or only some areas are gloss while others that should are not
- (some) parts of the image that should be gloss are missing entirely from the printout
Typical reasons include:
- wrong spot color name
- no spot color - none at all or mistakenly configured as process color instead of spot color
- overprint fill/stroke not enabled
- spot color objects placed below design

In all of these cases you can already see there is an issue at the RIP - e.g. in Thrive's Job Editor you can check the presence or absence of each color plane in a specific area (e.g. hover areas where you expect gloss and make sure both gloss & CMYK data is present before printing).
TT19 Flexfinish+ jobs arrive on UI with unexpected media width
For FLXfinish+ & the white print modes an additional 15mm is needed for the spitbar.
When printing an image center aligned, the image is centered on the media, not within the printable are.

If you want to be able to print the image and avoid clipping you will need to reserve space for 30mm instead of 15mm, otherwise the spitbar will not fit in the remaining space and the engine will be forced to clip the image.

Because of this, when submitting a job that requests center alignment without specifying the physical width, it can happen that the engine asks for a larger media than expected to accommodate for these extra 15mm.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
The fibers of banners can pollute the matrix plate. The fibers are visible in the print as small stripes in the print (hairs) Most of the banners are reinforced with fibers (e.g. IJM652, IJM673).
First automatic maintenance via the operator panel, if that not solve the issue, then manual maintenance.
Size detection and calibrations might fail if media is not centered.
Some film media can be electrically charged after being wound on the take-up roll.
Sometimes lines are visible on the media when roll position 2 is used.
Especially MPI3000 HOP (high opaque) is sensitive to mechanical contact with the 'bumps' in the media guide plate in the drawer.
Media with limited reflection of the backside cannot be detected by the optical media path sensors.
Use roll position 1 in case the guide plate in the drawer gives lines on the media.
Xtran wheel visible in the print.

Self-adhesive vinyl's, especially cast vinyl's are sensitive to Xtran Wheel stamps during printing. Slower print modes are less sensitive to Xtran stamping because the relaxation time (Xtran to first time ink on medium) is larger.
Use other media not sensitive for Xtran wheels or use a slower print mode.
Humidity sensitive media can be deformed after being exposed to humidity for a couple of hours.
Cut off 1 m of media before starting to print to avoid head touches after being exposed to humidity for a longer time.
XMS_MEDIA_SE strange behavior while calibration.
The X-tran media sensor can be influenced by TL light resulting in false positives.
Move the machine or put sheet of paper on right position on cover.
Media side edge fold on retraction of media.
Sometimes the side edge of the media is at an unfortunate position in the pinch, leading to a small fold of the side edge during retracting the media.
Position the media roll a couple of mm more to the left or to the right on the roll holder in the media drawer.
Media stuck on frame during printing.
Low humidity in combination with static sensitive media can lead to static problems, media can get stuck on frame during printing, leading to runnabilty problems.
Media step errors on 2nd side double sided print.
Double sided only available in matt print modes.
Loose winding media calibration scenario missing for inside printing: printhead touch/crash
Inside printing on loose winding media might generate a head touch on the leading edge due to upward curl of the media.
Use the locked tension bar for such media or use the feed button to feed the media to the taping position.
Unable to rewind media in locked tension bar mode.
The winder cannot spool backward.
As a result spool back is restricted by the length of the blouse.
Error 5713 Cannot read markers ->Printing on second side not possible when using max image width in combination with markers.
For double sided printing, the image must be at least 20 mm smaller than the printable area, to prevent that AAC markers are printed on the print surface due to skewing of the medium.
[PR2019-01231] Buffer plate marking back of media.
In case of buffer plate marking, buffer plate can be covered by a non-polluted smooth material, preferable Teflon tape.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around

Printing on the leading edge is not allowed with some media (stiff media, having a strong curl) since there is a risk that the print carriage hits the media during width detection. In case of loose winding, a leading strip of at least 700 mm is necessary to prevent that the print carriage hits the media during width detection.
Printing on the leading edge is not allowed, use (locked) tension bar media only.
Create specific media profiles for inside printing.
Do not use sticky media.

section.title: Remaining media length / End of roll handling

entry.p: Known problem
Additional information
Work around
The remaining length after startup is unknown.
Only after the roll has been used for printing, the remaining media length is shown.
Sometimes the remaining media length is not immediately reset to unknown after loading new media.
When a media type is used for the very first time, it takes 20% of the thickness of the roll before the remaining media length is accurately known.
Until that moment 'unknown' is shown.
For every next roll of that medium it takes about 1.5 m of printing before the remaining media length is known.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Diameter of wound-up roll is limited. Trailing edge can touch the floor after cutting.
Media can be slightly damaged by side-guards of the winder.
Media that are configured for use with tight winding or locked tension bar, cannot be used in free fall mode.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Pin cure mirrors can damage when print carriage crashes in a side edge of a very stiff media (like canvas).
After a media crash inspect the mirror box for visible damage.
Limited error handling after media crash or partial wipe.
When opening a cover during a pressure pulse wipe, the wipe might not be finished.
This leads to system pollution.
Possible lamp current error when opening top cover immediately after closing.
When opening the top cover immediately after closing, a lamp error can occur.
During error recovery, the "i" button with the errorcode are sometimes not shown.
Job does not resume after recovering error.
If both rolls are unloaded after an MRE, resume does not work and no media request is shown.
LUI keeps showing "Operator intervention required" after replacing maintenance tray.

LUI keeps showing "Operator intervention required" after replacing maintenance tray In a case an ORE is resolved, the UI will continue to show "Operator intervention/interaction required" until the engine is warmed up and reaches a stable state. As soon as the engine reaches standby and printheads and print surface (platen) have the correct temperature, the UI interaction will be removed.
When crash sensor of cure carriage is triggered at the beginning of the crash sensor check

Incorrect mre 2250009 "48V PSU cannot be switched on" when crash sensor is activated during initialization of the curing function. the 48V can not be switched on and the engine will report error 2250009 "48V PSU cannot be switched on" instead of "RightCrashSensorNotWorking".
Please check the cure carriage crash sensor.
Known problem
Additional information
Work around
Installing new software and mounting used printheads in the same step, will result in error 21522 (inconsistent serial Number).
Do not swap printheads and re-install the system at the same time. If printheads are swapped, restart the system first, so the serial number can be written in the printheads.
Setting change in the local UI, this is not updated into Datalog before a restart.
Settings modified from the printer local UI are updated in the datalog after a restart only.
The diagnostic cannot be exported to a USB SAM key with storage.
Unplug the SAM key, plug in a USB flash drive and export the diagnostic.
Error11504 when retrieving data dump.
Restart the engine and retrieve the data dump.
Not able to retrieve datalog.
When requesting a datalog generation or enter service mode while reboot for an engine MRE is being done, datalog cannot be retrieved.
Reboot the complete system.
Progress saving logfiles to USB-stick cannot be seen.

When you save logfiles to USB stick, this can take very long. If you, in the meantime, want to do something else and exit the screen where it states "saving" and come back again, the saving message is not there anymore.
Need to unplug - re-plug USB stick each time a new logging is made.
When exporting a tracelog to a USB key, the key is automatically ejected after the logfiles have been written.
If you have to write new logfiles the USB key, re-plug the USB key first.
Media width for service media is not preserved after restart.
Widths of the service media are not persistent over reboot. You need to re-enter them manually after each restart
After restart, the calibration of the service media is lost.
Perform the media calibration again.
Configured media thickness for service test media not shown in SDS tests.
Changing the media thickness of service media, via SDS test 251004, 251005 or 251006 is effectuate after the media is (re)loaded or reconfigured.
PrintCycleId starting from 1 in functional logging.
PrintCycleId starts at 1 after a reboot.
Error when importing TSM via USB.

Error when importing TSM via USB [PR2018-00977] When importing the TSM via USB, make sure that import is finished before you navigate in the LUI, else TSM import is not complete and you will get an error. press the Canon logo at the upper right section of the error screen to finalize import
Press the Canon logo at the upper right section of the error screen to finalize import.
No indication for the auto-import of the on-board TSM in C1630.

No indication for the auto-import of the on-board TSM in C1630 After first installation of the software, the on-board TSM needs to be imported. This is triggered by pressing the Refresh button in the settings menu of the TSM section in ADAM. Normally a red popup should appear in the upper right corner, indicating that the auto-import is in progress. This popup is sometimes not shown. The auto-import is however running. After 10-15 minutes, the auto-import is complete and the TSM is available.
Unreadable/mixed-up text on Remote Connection screen.
When the remote connection is lost, LUI shows a screen with unreadable and mixed-up text.
Remote assistance session timeout cannot be updated.
Disable remote assistance, save w/o exiting System settings, enable remote assistance again. You can then modify the timeout.
After an MRE it's not possible to start engine in ZERO SDS.
Switch the machine off and on again via the power button, and then go to zero SDS during starting up.
Crash sensor not connected results in loop in error recovery.
In case the connection with the carriage crash sensor is lost, the error "Left/Right crash sensor not working" is reported in an endless loop until the problem is solved.
Error 21752 Ink aging in printheads detected.

Automatic maintenance cannot be performed while the system is SDS; print heads should not be heated too long without maintenance when not printing. Therefore, if SDS is left open for more than 1 hour error 21752 (Ink aging detected) will occur.
After starting up the machine in zero SDS and pressing the 'Service user mode' button, the normal UI screen is shown. On the top line it says forever "Wait until the system is ready".
Reason for this is that the machine stays in Cold SDS (intended behavior).
In order to start the engine, you have to go to "Service" and then switch to warm SDS.
Jobs are not printed anymore after white print test.
First finish the white print SDS test and then start the a job.
SDS tests that print a test chart keep starting forever when print heads are not calibrated.
In case a printhead has been replaced, the printhead calibration must be executed. In case this is not done and a Service Print is started, the test remains in "Starting" without proper feedback.
Stop the test. Go to Service User Mode and perform the print head calibration.
Drawer led is off.
After you run the SDS test for the drawer LED, the LED will remain OFF, even if there is no media in the media path.
Deviating SDS-test 4-6-002 results.

Running SDS test 4-6-002 "get nozzle status", when SDS mode has been enabled for long, could produce false results (>300 dirt nozzles per head). This is caused by that service mode, interrupts normal maintenance behavior, which means nozzles can dry out.
First make a print, or perform an Automatic maintenance procedure.
Performing powerful pressure pulses and sending a print job from onyx results in pollution throughout the machine.

When performing an SDS cleaning action via "powerful pressure pulses", it is still possible to send a job via the RIP. When a job is sent, the printer starts printing while performing powerful pressure pulses; This results in severe pollution of the machine.
Do NOT send jobs when performing this test. or better Set Queue on Pause.
The printhead calibration (SDS test 4-6-026) in the C1630 takes about 14 minutes. In most cases, the initial installation of a machine will add another 20 minutes.
This is one-off and has to do with the temperature calibration.
This does not have to be the case when installing new heads in a used machine. This is because the engine already has temperature setpoints that are correct for the inks.
The test ED_CLE_PRESS_SE does not work.
Use ED_TEST_PE_SPE_CHECK_PRESSURE_LEAKAGE to display the current pressure.
Internal name used instead error description in error view in SDS.
In the error overview, the error description is not used. Instead an internal name is used.
ADAM: Info in "Repair & Evaluate" section is not preserved after restart.

ADAM: Info in "Repair & Evaluate" section is not preserved after restart Entered data in ADAM is saved when the ADAM user switches to another panel (Collect, Analyze, repair&Evaluate, TSM or System Service). Data entered without leaving the panel therefore is lost when the connection is interrupted.
Failed to load worklist in ADAM.
Close ADAM after the error and start it again.

When the DRCP board is exchanged with another one with different counters, the counters are temporarily wrong. To avoid decrease of volumePAC, the Wibu key must be removed when the software starts up after exchanging the DRCP board.
To avoid decrease of volumePAC, the Wibu key must be removed when the software starts up after exchanging the DRCP board.
The entered info in ADAM is not preserved after hard restart
Exit ADAM before doing a power down and Restore last session when starting ADAM again.
ADAM: Pressing a URL to a website leads to full blank screen

When viewing a TSM in ADAM on the console of a product, external links in the TSM are not accessible. If you try to access an external website the page is not loaded and the screen remains empty. If there is no back button try swiping the page to the right (this re-opens ADAM).
Pollution found on matrixplate
Protruded fibers will be cleaned by tissue, non protruded fibers are functional harmless.
When replacing a printhead it is wise to clean those fibers on the matrixplate.
ADAM: Weird values are displayed for print mode names and ink expiry dates.
In ADAM, weird values are shown for the following items:
- Expiry date of the in in the intermediate ink reservoir; the date is not shown correctly
- printModeName parameters: is shown as a list of numbers. The values are the decimal ASCII delimited with ;
Print mode names are already wrong in the datalog:
<parameters>
etc.
Same for ink expiry data:
<parameter name="21003" description="Expiry date of the ink in the intermediate ink reservoir" value="122;11;17"/>
ADAM simply shows the resources as logged in the datalog, this is a generic mechanism. There is no way to exclude specific entries from ADAM.
System goes to low-power mode during printing demo prints
When printing of the demo prints takes more than half an hour, the UI panel will show that the system went to low-power mode. The printing of the demo print continues however.
When pressing the UI panel, the system wakes up again.
Cannot change print surface temperature in media catalogue

Multiple instances of a printmode, with different printmode settings isn't possible in the current media synchronization architecture between RIP and Printer. Only one instance of a print mode is synchronized and overrides the others.
Create different medias in the RIP to secure the different setting (like print surface temperature)
Protruded fibers on matrixplate
Protruded fibers will be cleaned by tissue, non protruded fibers are functional harmless. When replacing a printhead it is wise to clean those fibers.
TSM: Saved annotations can't be deleted
Due to an issue in Robin/Portos TSM annotations can't be deleted.
Indicated print time on demo print is not correct
The indicated print time on the tickets of the demo prints is in UTC, so the Timezone is NOT taken into account.
Datalog and ORS contain info regarding the white printhead although it is not present
The permanent removal of the white printhead is not supported.
When the machine is equipped with a white printhead and it is afterwards removed, the Datalog and ORS will still contain info regarding the white printhead.
MaintenanceTrayEstimate restored wrongly

During restoring a backup, the ink level of the maintenance tray will be overwritten with the value of the backup in case this value is higher compared to the current level. This will then cause a jump in the maintenance tray level, shown on the UI.
To correct this you must replace the maintenance tray by the maintenance procedure triggered from the UI.
Permanent error 21571 after downgrade from R4.2 to R4.1
Downgrading a R4.2 machine will lead to a permanent error 21571.
Replace the Carriage PBA with a new one.
The entered info in ADAM 1.4.x is not preserved after hard restart
Exit ADAM before doing a power down and restore last session when starting ADAM again.
